absolute_flux_promotion_requirements.claim_boundary: This section defines the evidence needed to reopen absolute-flux promotion; it is not a promoted runtime/TOML predictor.

gates.detail: the current one-constant absolute calibration report is still failed
absolute-flux promotion needs a calibrated report that passes the held-out transport gate
holdouts must be passed, post-transient nonlinear transport windows outside the training split
external-VMEC holdout families test transfer beyond built-in local cases
at least one external stellarator-like VMEC holdout is required before broad stellarator absolute-flux claims
model-selection skill can pass while absolute-flux promotion remains blocked

notes: Admitted means the nonlinear window is already present in the current train/holdout metadata with a passed input/convergence gate. Excluded means the tracked nonlinear artifact is outside the current quasilinear absolute-flux holdout set or fails a gate. Next-best candidates are metadata-ranked only; the tool does not rerun simulations.

promotion_gate.reason: This artifact reports holdout readiness gaps only. The scoped model-selection gate may pass, but no runtime/TOML absolute-flux predictor is promoted.

screening_promotion_requirements.claim_boundary: This section defines what is missing before correlation-screening promotion; it does not promote absolute nonlinear heat-flux prediction.

gates.detail: useful model-development signal on the admitted portfolio
gates.required: at least one scoped screening model

gates.detail: held-out-only rank/correlation gate must pass before screening promotion

gates.detail: absolute-flux promotion remains a separate stricter target

gates.detail: more independent nonlinear holdouts reduce rank/correlation fragility
